extra.message: SQL query is built via Python string formatting ('%(name)s' is interpolated with the % operator before being passed to cur.execute). The `name` value originates from user-controlled POST data in sqli/views.py:57 (Student.create(conn, data['name'])) and is concatenated directly into the SQL statement, enabling SQL injection.

Jinja2 is configured with autoescape=False globally. Combined with templates that render user-controlled data without the |e filter (e.g. course.title, course.description, review.review_text in sqli/templates/course.jinja2, student name in sqli/templates/students.jinja2), this enables stored/reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

CSRF middleware is commented out of the middleware chain. State-changing POST endpoints (login, create student, create course, create review, evaluate) are therefore not protected against Cross-Site Request Forgery.

Passwords are hashed using unsalted MD5 (hashlib.md5). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage; combined with the absence of a salt it allows trivial rainbow table / brute-force attacks on leaked hashes.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Redis session storage is initialized with httponly=False, exposing the session cookie to JavaScript. Combined with the XSS issues enabled by autoescape=False, this allows attackers to steal session cookies via document.cookie.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag

extra.message: The `students` handler accepts POST requests to create new Student records without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can create students.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `courses` handler accepts POST requests to create new Course records without any authentication/authorization check (despite the template only showing the form to admins, the endpoint itself is unprotected).

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `review` handler allows any unauthenticated user to POST reviews for any course. No authentication/authorization check is performed, enabling anonymous stored content injection (which combined with autoescape=False leads to stored XSS).

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `evaluate` handler creates Mark records (student grades) without any authentication/authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can assign grades to any student for any course — a broken access control / privilege escalation flaw.
aiohttp Application is started with debug=True. In production this can disclose stack traces and internal information to remote users.
